Complete Furnace Maintenance Checklist for Stony Plain & Spruce Grove Homes

Here's exactly what your Parkland County furnace needs to survive Alberta winters:

How to check your filter: Hold it up to light. Can't see through it? It needs replacing. During peak winter (November-February), check every 3 weeks.

Best time for Stony Plain & Spruce Grove residents: Early September, before the fall rush. By mid-October, every furnace company in Parkland County is backed up with last-minute calls.

  • Flame Sensor: A dirty sensor causes nuisance shutdowns on the coldest nights
  • Condensate Drain: Essential for high-efficiency furnaces common in newer Spruce Grove homes

Even the cleanest furnace can't perform well with clogged ducts. According to NADCA (National Air Duct Cleaners Association), professional cleaning improves system efficiency and indoor air quality. Signs you need duct cleaning in Stony Plain/Spruce Grove:

  • Homes with pets: Every 2-3 years
  • Acreages/farms: Every 2-3 years (agricultural dust)
  • Post-renovation: Immediately (drywall dust destroys systems)

🔥 Quick Safety Alert for Stony Plain & Spruce Grove Homeowners:

15,000+ dryer fires occur annually in North America — the National Fire Protection Association identifies clogged dryer vents as the leading cause, with clogged vents being the #1 cause. Stony Plain and Spruce Grove homes should have dryer vents professionally cleaned every 12 months (every 6 months for large families). This simple maintenance prevents 90% of dryer fires. Call Home Pros Group at (780) 932-7337 to protect your Parkland County family today.

The Hidden Fire Hazard in Your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove Laundry Room

Most homeowners in Stony Plain and Spruce Grove don't realize their clothes dryer is one of the most dangerous appliances in their home. Every 22 minutes, a dryer fire occurs somewhere in North America. In Parkland County, where many homes have longer vent runs due to basement laundry rooms and Alberta's cold climate construction, the risk is even higher.

What makes dryer vent fires particularly dangerous? They often start behind walls or in attics where they're invisible until it's too late. By the time you smell smoke, the fire may have already spread through your home's structure.

📍 We Serve All Parkland County:

  • Stony Plain (Heritage Park, West Haven, Hilldowns, Westerra neighborhoods)
  • Spruce Grove (Woodgrove, Grove Pointe, King Street area, Campsite Road)
  • Parkland County rural areas (Wabamun, Duffield, Entwistle, Seba Beach)
  • Acreages throughout Parkland County

How Dryer Vent Fires Start in Stony Plain & Spruce Grove Homes

Understanding the mechanics of dryer fires helps you recognize the warning signs specific to Parkland County homes:

Stage 1: Lint Accumulation

Even when you clean your lint trap religiously (and you should!), 25-30% of lint bypasses the trap and enters your vent system. In Stony Plain and Spruce Grove homes with:

  • Basement laundry rooms: Vents often run 15-30 feet to reach exterior walls
  • Multiple 90° bends: Common in older Stony Plain homes, each bend traps more lint
  • Winter condensation: Alberta's extreme cold can cause moisture buildup that makes lint stick to vent walls

Stage 2: Airflow Restriction

As lint accumulates over months, it restricts airflow. Your dryer compensates by working harder and running hotter. This is why clothes that used to dry in 45 minutes in your Spruce Grove home now take 90+ minutes.

Stage 3: Heat Buildup

Trapped heat can't escape through the clogged vent. Internal temperatures that should be 60-70°C can spike to 150-200°C. In Parkland County's well-insulated, tightly sealed homes, this heat has nowhere to go.

Stage 4: Ignition

Lint is incredibly flammable. Health Canada recommends regular appliance maintenance to prevent household fire hazards. When temperatures reach 180-200°C, spontaneous combustion occurs. The fire spreads rapidly through the accumulated lint in your vent system, often inside walls where it's impossible to see or smell until significant damage is done.

⚠️ Special Risk for Parkland County Acreages:

Rural properties near Stony Plain, Duffield, and Wabamun often have vents running 30-50+ feet to exterior walls. These long runs accumulate lint faster and are harder to clean with DIY methods. Professional cleaning is essential. Call (780) 932-7337 for rural dryer vent service.

Warning Signs Your Stony Plain/Spruce Grove Dryer Vent is Dangerous

Don't wait for a fire. Watch for these critical warning signs common in Parkland County homes:

1. Dramatically Longer Drying Times

Normal: A full load of laundry dries in 30-45 minutes.
Warning sign: Same load now takes 60-90+ minutes or requires 2-3 cycles.

This is the #1 indicator Stony Plain and Spruce Grove homeowners report before dryer fires. Don't ignore it!

2. Burning Smell During Operation

Any burning smell—even if it's faint—is an emergency. Stop using your dryer immediately and schedule professional vent cleaning. This smell means lint inside your vent system is approaching ignition temperature.

3. Dryer Exterior is Too Hot to Touch

During a cycle, touch the top and sides of your dryer. It should be warm but comfortable to touch. If it's uncomfortably hot or you can't keep your hand on it for more than 2-3 seconds, heat isn't escaping properly.

4. Laundry Room Feels Like a Sauna

Your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove laundry room shouldn't feel tropical during dryer operation. Excessive heat and humidity indicate poor ventilation—moisture and heat are being pushed back into your home instead of exhausting outside.

5. Lint Accumulation Around Dryer Door Seal

Visible lint around the door, on the floor, or on nearby surfaces suggests lint is escaping because it can't exit through the clogged vent. This is common in Parkland County homes with restricted vents.

6. Outdoor Vent Flap Doesn't Open

Go outside while your dryer runs. The exterior vent flap (often located on the side of your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home) should open with the force of exhausting air. If it stays closed, barely moves, or only opens slightly, you have a significant blockage.

Winter tip: In Alberta winters, check that snow, ice, or frost haven't blocked your exterior vent. This is especially common after heavy snowfalls or during prolonged cold snaps below -20°C.

7. Clothes Are Still Damp But HOT

If clothes come out of the dryer hot to the touch but still damp, your dryer is producing heat but can't exhaust the moisture. This is a classic sign of vent restriction in Spruce Grove homes.

8. Musty Smell on Dried Clothes

Clothes that smell musty or mildewed after drying indicate moisture isn't being removed properly due to poor ventilation. This is common in Parkland County homes with partially blocked vents.

The Financial Cost of Clogged Dryer Vents in Parkland County

Beyond the life-threatening fire risk, clogged dryer vents drain your wallet:

Energy Waste

Extended drying times in Stony Plain and Spruce Grove homes can double your dryer's electricity consumption. For a typical household doing 5-6 loads weekly:

  • Clean vent: ~$10-15/month in electricity
  • Clogged vent: $20-30/month (running 2-3 cycles per load)
  • Annual waste: $120-180 in unnecessary energy costs

Appliance Damage

Overworked dryers fail sooner. Components that fail prematurely on restricted dryers include:

  • Heating element: $150-300 to replace
  • Thermal fuse: $100-200 service call
  • Blower motor: $200-400 repair
  • Complete replacement: $800-1,500+ for new dryer

Professional dryer vent cleaning costs a fraction of these repairs and prevents premature failure.

Clothing Damage

Excessive heat from overworked dryers degrades fabric, causing:

  • Premature wear and fading
  • Elastic breakdown (waistbands, cuffs)
  • Shrinkage beyond normal levels
  • Heat damage to delicate fabrics

Time Waste

Running 2-3 cycles for loads that should take one cycle wastes hours every week. For busy Parkland County families, time is precious.

DIY Dryer Vent Maintenance for Stony Plain & Spruce Grove Homeowners

After Every Load: Clean the Lint Trap

This is non-negotiable. Remove lint from the trap before or after every single load. Never run your dryer without the lint trap installed.

Monthly deep clean: Wash your lint screen with soap and hot water. Fabric softener sheets leave an invisible film that restricts airflow. Rinse thoroughly and air dry before reinstalling.

Monthly: Vacuum Behind Your Dryer

Pull your dryer away from the wall and vacuum:

  • Behind and underneath the dryer
  • The vent connection point at the back of the dryer
  • The floor around the dryer (lint accumulates everywhere)

Quarterly: Inspect Your Exterior Vent

Walk outside and locate your dryer vent (common locations in Stony Plain/Spruce Grove homes: side exterior walls, sometimes rear walls for walkout basements).

  • Remove any visible lint from the exterior vent opening
  • Ensure the vent flap opens and closes freely
  • Check for bird nests or wasp nests (common in spring/summer)
  • Clear any snow, ice, or frost in winter months

What DIY Can't Do

Home vacuums and hardware store vent cleaning kits have serious limitations for Parkland County homes:

  • Household vacuums lack power: Can't pull lint from 15-30+ foot vent runs common in Stony Plain/Spruce Grove basements
  • Flexible brush kits can make it worse: If used incorrectly, they push lint deeper into the system, creating a compacted blockage
  • Can't access full vent length: Especially vents running through walls, floors, or to second-story laundry rooms
  • Can't handle complex configurations: Vents with multiple bends, vertical runs, or routing around obstacles

💡 Home Pros Group Professional Cleaning:

We use commercial-grade rotary brush systems and high-powered vacuums that completely remove lint from the entire vent run—from your dryer to the exterior wall. Our equipment handles even the longest vents common in Parkland County acreages and basement laundry rooms.

Flat-rate pricing. No surprises. Call (780) 932-7337 for your free quote. Serving Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and all of Parkland County since 2018.

Professional Dryer Vent Cleaning: What to Expect in Stony Plain & Spruce Grove

The Process

  1. Inspection: We assess your vent configuration, length, material type, and identify problem areas
  2. Disconnection: Dryer is carefully pulled away from the wall and disconnected from the vent
  3. High-Powered Extraction: Commercial vacuum and rotary brush system removes ALL lint from the entire vent run (we handle vents up to 50+ feet for rural Parkland properties)
  4. Exterior Cleaning: Outside vent is thoroughly cleaned and inspected for damage, nests, or winter blockages
  5. Reconnection: Dryer is reconnected with properly sealed, code-compliant connections
  6. Airflow Testing: We verify proper exhaust airflow to ensure safety and efficiency
  7. Recommendations: Honest assessment of vent condition and any repairs needed

How Often Should Stony Plain & Spruce Grove Homes Schedule Cleaning?

  • Standard households (2-4 loads/week): Annually
  • Large families (5+ loads/week): Every 6 months
  • Homes with pets: Every 6-9 months (pet hair accelerates buildup)
  • Vents longer than 25 feet: Every 6-9 months (common in Parkland County homes)
  • Vents with 3+ bends/elbows: Every 6-9 months
  • Acreages with 40+ foot vents: Every 6 months
  • If you notice ANY warning signs: Immediately, regardless of last cleaning date

Special Considerations for Parkland County Homes

Alberta Winter Challenges

Stony Plain and Spruce Grove's harsh winters create unique dryer vent issues:

Ice and Frost Buildup

When -25°C outdoor air meets warm, moist exhaust from your dryer, ice forms rapidly at the exterior vent. This can:

  • Block exhaust flow completely
  • Force moisture back into your home
  • Create a fire hazard as your dryer overheats

Solution: Check your exterior vent weekly during cold snaps. Gently remove any ice buildup. Consider upgrading to an insulated vent hood designed for Alberta winters.

Vent Flap Freezing

Exterior vent flaps can freeze shut during prolonged cold weather. If the flap doesn't open, exhaust can't escape.

Solution: Test your vent flap weekly in winter. If it's frozen, carefully thaw it (warm water in a spray bottle works well). Never force it open—you'll break the hinge.

Snow Drifts

Parkland County's prairie winds create deep snow drifts that can completely bury exterior vents, especially on north-facing walls.

Solution: After heavy snowfalls, check and clear your exterior vent. Mark its location with a stake so you can find it in deep snow.

Multi-Story and Acreage Homes

If your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home has:

  • Second-floor laundry room
  • Basement laundry with long vent runs
  • Acreage with 40-60+ foot vents
  • Multiple 90° bends in vent routing

You're at higher risk for lint buildup and require more frequent professional cleaning (every 6 months minimum). DIY methods simply can't handle these configurations safely or effectively.

DIY kits from hardware stores can't match the power of professional rotary brush systems and truck-mounted vacuums, especially for Parkland County homes with long vent runs and complex configurations.

What if my dryer vent hasn't been cleaned in years?

If you've lived in your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home for 3+ years and never had your dryer vent professionally cleaned, schedule service immediately. Long-neglected vents pose serious fire risks. Stop using your dryer if you notice warning signs (burning smell, excessive heat, dramatically longer drying times) and call for emergency vent cleaning. Does dryer vent cleaning also include duct cleaning for my furnace?

No, these are separate services. Dryer vent cleaning addresses the exhaust duct from your clothes dryer to the exterior of your home. title: Indoor Air Quality Stony Plain & Spruce Grove: Complete Health Guide for Parkland County Homes 2026
excerpt: Improve your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home's air quality. Expert solutions for Alberta's climate challenges, HVAC maintenance, and healthier living in Parkland County.
author: Home Pros Group - Stony Plain
featured_image_alt: Family breathing clean indoor air in their Stony Plain home after professional duct cleaning

meta_description: Breathe cleaner air in your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home. Expert IAQ solutions for Parkland County. Professional HVAC cleaning. Call (780) 932-7337!
content:
💨 Quick Answer for Stony Plain & Spruce Grove Residents:

Indoor air in Parkland County homes can be 2-5 times more polluted than outdoor air — consistent with Health Canada's indoor air quality guidelines, especially during Alberta's 6-month heating season. Poor air quality causes allergies, asthma, headaches, and long-term health issues. The solution? Annual furnace cleaning, duct cleaning every 3-5 years, proper filtration, and ventilation. Call Home Pros Group at (780) 932-7337 for professional HVAC cleaning in Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County.

Why Indoor Air Quality is Critical in Stony Plain & Spruce Grove

Residents of Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County spend 90% of their time indoors—especially during Alberta's brutal winters that last from October through April. When it's -30°C outside, opening windows for fresh air isn't an option.

Here's the problem: modern Parkland County homes are built to be energy-efficient with tight building envelopes that trap heat. They also trap indoor air pollutants. Without proper ventilation and HVAC maintenance, you're breathing the same contaminated air day after day, month after month.

📍 Proudly Serving Parkland County:

  • Stony Plain (Heritage Park, West Haven, Eden Drive, Hilldowns, all neighborhoods)
  • Spruce Grove (Woodgrove, King Street area, Campsite Road, Jennifer Heil Way, Grove Pointe)
  • Parkland County rural communities (Wabamun, Duffield, Entwistle, Seba Beach, Tomahawk)
  • Acreages and farmhouses throughout Parkland County

Common Indoor Air Pollutants in Stony Plain & Spruce Grove Homes

1. Dust and Dust Mites

Why it's worse here: Stony Plain and Spruce Grove's prairie location means homes are exposed to agricultural dust, pollen from surrounding farmland, and particles carried by constant winds. Alberta's extremely dry winter air (often below 15% humidity) causes materials to break down into finer dust that stays airborne longer.

Health effects:

  • Allergic reactions (sneezing, itchy eyes, congestion)
  • Asthma triggers and attacks
  • Respiratory irritation and chronic cough
  • Sleep disruption

2. Pet Dander

Many Parkland County families have dogs and cats. During winter, when pets spend more time indoors and windows stay closed, dander concentrations spike.

Sources in your Stony Plain/Spruce Grove home:

  • Pet fur and skin cells in carpets and furniture
  • Circulated continuously through HVAC system
  • Concentrated in bedrooms where pets sleep

Health effects: Allergic reactions, asthma exacerbation, chronic nasal congestion

3. Volatile Organic Compounds (VOCs)

VOCs are gases emitted from everyday products common in every Parkland County home:

  • Cleaning products (Windex, Lysol, Pine-Sol)
  • Air fresheners and candles (popular in tightly sealed winter homes)
  • Paints, varnishes, and stains
  • New furniture and carpets (off-gassing for 6-12 months)
  • Building materials (especially in newer Spruce Grove developments)
  • Pressed wood products (formaldehyde release)

Health effects: Headaches, dizziness, nausea, eye/nose/throat irritation. Long-term exposure linked to liver and kidney damage.

4. Mold Spores

While Stony Plain and Spruce Grove's low humidity limits widespread mold growth, problem areas exist in nearly every home:

  • Bathrooms after showers (especially without proper ventilation)
  • Basements (common in Parkland County homes, especially older properties)
  • Around windows (winter condensation from temperature differentials)
  • HVAC systems (dark, sometimes damp environments perfect for growth)
  • Under sinks (slow plumbing leaks)

Health effects: Respiratory issues, allergic reactions, asthma attacks, weakened immune response, chronic sinus infections

5. Carbon Monoxide (CO)

This is a critical concern for Stony Plain and Spruce Grove homes with gas furnaces (the majority of Parkland County homes). CO is odorless, colorless, and deadly.

Sources:

  • Poorly maintained or dirty gas furnaces
  • Cracked heat exchangers (common in furnaces 15+ years old)
  • Blocked furnace flues or chimneys
  • Attached garages (vehicle exhaust seeping into living spaces)
  • Gas stoves and fireplaces without proper ventilation

Health effects: Headaches, dizziness, nausea, confusion, unconsciousness, death at high concentrations

⚠️ CO Safety is Life or Death:

Every Stony Plain and Spruce Grove home with a gas furnace MUST have working CO detectors on every floor. Test them monthly. Replace batteries annually. Replace the entire detector every 7 years.

Annual professional furnace cleaning and inspection catches issues like cracked heat exchangers BEFORE they become deadly. Call (780) 932-7337 to schedule your furnace inspection.

6. Radon

Parkland County has moderate to high radon risk due to uranium-bearing soil common in Alberta. Radon is a radioactive gas that enters homes through foundation cracks, sump pumps, and gaps around pipes.

Why Stony Plain/Spruce Grove residents should care: Radon is the #2 cause of lung cancer in Canada (after smoking) and the #1 cause among non-smokers.

Testing: Purchase a long-term radon test kit (3-12 months) for accurate readings. Test in your basement or lowest lived-in level. If levels exceed 200 Bq/m³, install a radon mitigation system.

How Parkland County's Climate Affects Your Indoor Air Quality

Extreme Temperature Swings

Stony Plain and Spruce Grove experience some of Alberta's most dramatic temperature variations: -40°C winter nights to +30°C summer days. This creates unique IAQ challenges:

  • Sealed Homes 6+ Months: From October to April, windows stay closed, trapping pollutants
  • HVAC System Strain: Furnaces run continuously for months, circulating dust and contaminants
  • Minimal Fresh Air Exchange: Without mechanical ventilation, stale air accumulates

Ultra-Low Winter Humidity

Winter indoor humidity in Parkland County homes often drops to 10-15% (drier than the Sahara Desert at 25%). This causes:

  • Dry, irritated airways more susceptible to infection
  • Static electricity that lifts and suspends dust particles in air
  • Increased dust generation from dry materials breaking down
  • Respiratory discomfort (bloody noses, sore throats, dry coughs)

Prairie Dust and Agricultural Particles

Stony Plain and Spruce Grove's location in Parkland County means exposure to:

  • Windblown dust from surrounding farmland
  • Pollen from agricultural crops
  • Particulates from gravel roads (especially rural acreages)
  • Grain dust during harvest season

All of these enter your home through HVAC air intake, open doors, and on clothing/shoes.

Wildfire Smoke Season

Alberta's summers increasingly bring wildfire smoke from BC and northern Alberta. Parkland County sees multiple days each summer with poor Air Quality Index (AQI) readings. Without proper HVAC filtration, outdoor smoke infiltrates your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home.

Signs of Poor Indoor Air Quality in Your Parkland County Home

Many IAQ problems produce subtle symptoms Stony Plain and Spruce Grove residents dismiss as "normal Alberta living." They're not normal—they're fixable.

Health Symptoms

  • Frequent headaches, especially in the morning or while at home
  • Chronic sinus congestion or runny nose (not related to colds)
  • Dry, irritated, or itchy eyes
  • Persistent cough without illness
  • Fatigue, brain fog, or difficulty concentrating at home
  • Worsening allergies or asthma symptoms indoors
  • Symptoms improve when you leave the house (critical indicator!)

Environmental Signs in Your Stony Plain/Spruce Grove Home

  • Excessive dust on furniture within 2-3 days of cleaning
  • Visible dust blowing from vents when furnace starts
  • Musty, stale, or stuffy smell (especially in basements)
  • Condensation on windows (indicates poor ventilation)
  • Visible mold growth (bathrooms, window frames, basement corners)
  • Uneven heating or cooling (some rooms always too hot/cold)
  • Pets scratching or showing allergy symptoms more than usual

Proven Solutions for Better Indoor Air Quality in Stony Plain & Spruce Grove

1. HVAC Maintenance (Most Important!)

Your furnace and duct system are the lungs of your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home. Neglect them, and you're breathing contaminated air with every cycle.

Essential HVAC Maintenance for Parkland County Homes:

Monthly Filter Replacement (October-April):

  • Use MERV 8-11 pleated filters minimum
  • MERV 13+ for allergy/asthma sufferers
  • Check filter every 3-4 weeks during heavy heating season
  • Don't use cheap fiberglass filters—they only protect equipment, not air quality

Annual Furnace Cleaning (September):

  • Removes accumulated dust, mold, and bacteria from blower compartments
  • Cleans heat exchanger (critical for CO safety)
  • Addresses areas DIY cleaning can't reach
  • Includes safety inspection for carbon monoxide risks
  • Improves efficiency by 10-20% (lower heating bills!)

Duct Cleaning Every 3-5 Years (per NADCA standards):

  • Eliminates years of accumulated dust, dander, and mold spores
  • Especially critical for Stony Plain/Spruce Grove homes with pets
  • Essential after renovations (drywall dust destroys air quality)
  • Recommended every 2-3 years for acreages exposed to agricultural dust

💡 Home Pros Group HVAC Services:

We're Stony Plain's local HVAC cleaning specialists. Flat-rate pricing. No vent counting. No hidden fees. We clean EVERY register in your home for one honest price—whether you have 10 vents or 30.

Professional furnace cleaning, duct cleaning, and dryer vent cleaning for Parkland County homes since 2018.

Call (780) 932-7337 for your free quote. Same-day and next-day service available.

2. Upgrade Your Air Filtration

Filter Selection Guide for Stony Plain/Spruce Grove Homes:

Filter Type MERV Rating What It Captures Best For
Basic Fiberglass 1-4 Large particles only Furnace protection (NOT air quality)
Pleated Standard 8-11 Dust, pollen, mold spores Most Parkland County homes
High-Efficiency 13-16 Bacteria, smoke, pet dander, VOCs Allergies, asthma, pets, wildfire season
HEPA 17-20 99.97% of all particles Severe allergies, immune issues

Important: Check your furnace specifications before upgrading to MERV 13+. Some older systems can't handle the increased airflow restriction. Ask your HVAC technician during your annual cleaning.

3. Control Humidity Levels (Critical in Alberta!)

Ideal indoor humidity for Stony Plain/Spruce Grove homes: 30-45%

Winter Problem: Too Low (10-20%)

Solutions:

  • Whole-home humidifier: Attaches to furnace, adds moisture to all heated air. Best solution for Parkland County homes.
  • Portable humidifiers: Place in bedrooms for overnight relief
  • Air-dry laundry indoors occasionally: Adds moisture naturally
  • Keep bathroom doors open after showers: Distributes moisture throughout home

Summer/Shoulder Season Problem: Too High (50%+)

Solutions:

  • Run bathroom exhaust fans 20+ minutes after showers
  • Use kitchen range hood when cooking (vent to outside, not recirculating)
  • Run dehumidifier in damp basements (common in older Stony Plain homes)
  • Fix plumbing leaks immediately
  • Improve ventilation (see next section)

4. Improve Ventilation

Even in -30°C winter, your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home needs fresh air exchange. Without it, you're re-breathing the same stale, contaminated air for months.

Solutions for Parkland County Homes:

Heat Recovery Ventilator (HRV):

  • Exchanges stale indoor air with fresh outdoor air
  • Recovers 70-90% of heat (critical in Alberta!)
  • Essential for new, tightly sealed Spruce Grove homes built after 2010
  • Operates continuously at low levels, no manual intervention needed

Exhaust Fans:

  • Bathroom fans: Run 20+ minutes after every shower (moisture removal prevents mold)
  • Kitchen range hood: Must vent to outside (not recirculating). Use when cooking.

Strategic Window Opening (Shoulder Seasons):

  • Even 5-10 minutes on milder days (-5°C or warmer) helps flush stale air
  • Open windows on opposite sides of home for cross-ventilation
  • Best times: Spring (April-May) and Fall (September-October)

5. Air Purifiers for Targeted Solutions

Portable air purifiers work well for specific rooms in your Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home:

  • HEPA purifiers: Capture 99.97% of airborne particles. Best for bedrooms and main living areas.
  • Activated carbon filters: Remove odors, VOCs, and smoke. Great for kitchens and pet areas.
  • UV purifiers: Kill bacteria and viruses. Often installed directly in HVAC systems.

Sizing tip: Choose a purifier rated for at least 1.5x your room's square footage for effective air exchanges (4-5 complete room air changes per hour).

6. Source Control (Most Effective!)

The best air quality improvement is eliminating pollutants at the source:

  • Switch to low-VOC products: Paints, cleaners, air fresheners (available at Stony Plain Home Hardware, Spruce Grove Home Depot)
  • Remove shoes at the door: Prevents outdoor pollutants, pesticides, and allergens from spreading. Critical for Parkland County's dusty/agricultural environment.
  • Groom pets regularly: Brush pets outdoors to reduce indoor dander
  • Store chemicals properly: Tightly sealed containers in garage or utility room, NOT living spaces
  • No smoking indoors: Tobacco smoke contains 7,000+ chemicals and lingers in fabrics for months
  • Minimize candle and incense use: Both produce particulate pollution

Frequently Asked Questions: Indoor Air Quality in Stony Plain & Spruce Grove

How do I test indoor air quality in my Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home?

You can purchase basic IAQ monitors ($100-300) that track temperature, humidity, CO2, VOCs, and PM2.5 particles. For comprehensive testing of mold, radon, specific allergens, and other pollutants, hire an IAQ professional. For Parkland County homes, we recommend at minimum: (1) CO detectors on every floor, (2) a long-term radon test kit for your basement, and (3) an indoor humidity monitor. These cover the most critical risks for our area.

Will opening windows in winter help my Spruce Grove home's air quality?

Yes, briefly. Even 5-10 minutes of fresh air exchange on milder winter days (-5°C or warmer) helps dilute indoor pollutants. However, this isn't practical as a primary solution for Parkland County's 6-month heating season. Invest in mechanical ventilation (HRV/ERV) for continuous fresh air without massive heat loss. This is the best solution for Stony Plain and Spruce Grove's climate.

How often should I have my ducts cleaned in Parkland County?

Every 3-5 years for most Stony Plain and Spruce Grove homes. More frequently (every 2-3 years) if you: have pets, recent renovations, live on an acreage with agricultural dust exposure, have family members with allergies/asthma, or smoke indoors (please don't!). If you've never had your ducts cleaned and have lived in your home 5+ years, schedule service now. Home Pros Group provides professional duct cleaning with transparent flat-rate pricing. Call (780) 932-7337.

What air filter should I use during wildfire smoke season in Stony Plain?

MERV 13 or higher for your furnace, or HEPA portable purifiers for key rooms. During wildfire smoke events (common in Parkland County summers), run your HVAC fan continuously (set to "on" not "auto") to keep air filtering through the system. Keep windows closed. Consider portable HEPA purifiers in bedrooms. Replace filters more frequently during heavy smoke periods—they'll clog faster.

We're locally owned and based right here in Stony Plain—not an Edmonton company that "also serves" the area. We understand Parkland County homes, Alberta's climate challenges, and the specific HVAC configurations common in our community. What makes us different: flat-rate pricing with no vent counting, no hidden fees, honest assessments (we'll never sell you services you don't need), same-day/next-day service available, and we've proudly served our Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County neighbors since 2018. content:
How Often Should You Clean Your Furnace in Alberta?

If you are an Alberta homeowner, your furnace is one of the hardest-working appliances in your home. From October through April — sometimes even longer — it runs almost continuously to keep your family warm through temperatures that regularly drop below -30°C. With that kind of workload, knowing how often to clean your furnace is not just a maintenance question — it is a financial and safety decision.

The Short Answer: Once a Year

For most homes in Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, Parkland County, and the Greater Edmonton area, we recommend annual furnace cleaning. This is consistent with NADCA guidelines and what most HVAC manufacturers recommend and what is needed to maintain your furnace warranty in many cases.

Annual cleaning ensures that dust, debris, and allergens that accumulate during the heating season are removed before they cause problems. It also gives a professional a chance to visually inspect your system and catch potential issues before they turn into expensive emergency repairs.

Why Alberta Homes Need More Frequent Cleaning

Compared to homeowners in milder Canadian climates like Vancouver or Victoria, Alberta homeowners put significantly more stress on their furnaces. Consider the math: if your furnace runs 6-7 months per year at high output, it is circulating air through your ductwork thousands of times each heating season. Every cycle pulls dust, pet dander, pollen, cooking particles, and other contaminants through the system.

In a single heating season, a typical Alberta furnace will push approximately 1,000 to 1,500 cubic metres of air through your ducts — and all the dust and debris that comes with it. By the time spring arrives, there is a noticeable buildup inside your ducts and on your furnace components.

When You Might Need Cleaning More Often

While annual cleaning is the baseline, some households benefit from cleaning every 8-10 months:

  • Pet owners: Dogs and cats shed constantly, and pet dander is one of the biggest contributors to duct contamination. If you have multiple pets, consider cleaning twice a year.
  • Allergy sufferers: If anyone in your household has asthma, allergies, or respiratory sensitivities, more frequent cleaning can make a real difference in symptom management.
  • Recent renovations: Drywall dust, sawdust, and construction debris spread through your duct system during renovations. Always get a cleaning after major renovations.
  • Smokers: Cigarette and cannabis smoke residue builds up in ducts and recirculates with every cycle.
  • Older homes: Homes built before 1990 often have more dust and debris issues due to aging ductwork and insulation.

The Cost of Skipping Furnace Cleaning

Some homeowners try to save money by skipping annual cleaning. Here is what that actually costs you:

  • Higher energy bills: A dirty furnace works up to 20% harder to heat your home. On a typical Alberta heating bill of $150-$250/month, that is $180-$300 extra per year.
  • Shorter furnace lifespan: A well-maintained furnace lasts 15-20 years. A neglected one often fails at 8-12 years. Replacement cost: $4,000-$8,000+.
  • Emergency repairs: 90% of furnace failures are caused by lack of maintenance. Emergency HVAC calls in Alberta run $500-$1,500+.
  • Health impacts: Dirty ducts circulate allergens, mold spores, and potentially harmful particles throughout your home.

Best Time to Schedule Your Cleaning

The ideal time for furnace cleaning in Alberta is late summer or early fall — September and October are perfect. This ensures your system is clean and ready before the heating season begins. However, we clean furnaces year-round, and any time is better than not cleaning at all.

Spring cleaning (April-May) is also popular, as it removes everything that accumulated during the winter before you switch to air conditioning or open windows for the summer.

What Does a Professional Furnace Cleaning Include?

At Home Pros Group, our $345 flat-rate cleaning covers:

  • All furnace components (burners, blower, motor, flame sensor)
  • Every supply and return duct in your home
  • All register covers
  • Main trunk lines and branch lines
  • Visual safety inspection
  • Filter check and recommendation

We do not charge per vent, and we never add hidden fees. Whether your home has 10 vents or 30, the price is the same.

Book Your Annual Cleaning Today

If it has been more than a year since your last furnace cleaning, now is the time to call. Home Pros Group serves Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, Parkland County, and Greater Edmonton with honest, flat-rate pricing. Call (780) 932-7337 for your free quote.

Seasonal Maintenance Checklist for Alberta Homeowners

Along with your annual furnace cleaning, here is a complete seasonal checklist to keep your home running smoothly through Alberta's extreme weather:

Fall (September-October)

  • Schedule annual furnace and duct cleaning with Home Pros Group
  • Replace furnace filter (start the season with a fresh MERV 11)
  • Test carbon monoxide detectors and replace batteries
  • Test smoke detectors throughout the home
  • Check weatherstripping around doors and windows
  • Clean gutters before freeze-up
  • Ensure outdoor dryer vent is clear of debris

Winter (December-February)

  • Check furnace filter monthly during peak usage
  • Keep supply and return vents clear of furniture and obstructions
  • Monitor carbon monoxide detectors regularly
  • Keep the area around your furnace clean and clear
  • Watch for signs of furnace trouble: unusual noises, uneven heating, strange odours

Spring (April-May)

  • Replace furnace filter after the winter season
  • Consider spring furnace cleaning if you missed the fall
  • Schedule dryer vent cleaning
  • Clean gutters after snow melt
  • Open windows to air out the house on warm days

Summer (June-August)

  • Book your fall furnace cleaning appointment now (avoid the September rush)
  • Replace furnace filter even though the system is running less
  • If you have central air conditioning, have the AC coils cleaned
  • This is the best time for any ductwork repairs or modifications

The Investment That Pays For Itself

At $345 for a complete furnace and duct cleaning, the annual cost is modest compared to the benefits. Consider: if dirty ducts are costing you an extra $180-$300 per year in heating bills, the cleaning pays for itself. Add in the extended furnace life (avoiding a $4,000-$8,000 replacement 5-10 years early) and the health benefits of cleaner air, and annual furnace cleaning is one of the best investments a homeowner can make.

Do not wait until something goes wrong. Be proactive. Call Home Pros Group at (780) 932-7337 and get your annual cleaning on the calendar.

What Does Duct Cleaning Actually Cost in Stony Plain and Spruce Grove?

If you have started shopping for duct cleaning in Stony Plain or Spruce Grove, you have probably noticed a huge range in pricing. Some companies advertise $99 specials. Others quote $500+. How do you know what is fair?

As a local duct cleaning company that has been serving Parkland County (and follows NADCA cleaning standards) since 2005, we are going to give you a completely transparent breakdown of duct cleaning costs — including the pricing tricks that many companies use to inflate your final bill.

The Two Pricing Models

There are two main ways duct cleaning companies price their services:

1. Per-Vent Pricing (Watch Out)

Most duct cleaning companies in the Edmonton area use per-vent pricing. They advertise a low base rate — typically $99-$149 — then add a charge for each vent (register) in your home. The per-vent charge usually ranges from $15-$25.

Here is how the math works for a typical Stony Plain or Spruce Grove home:

  • Base price: $99-$149
  • Average home has 15-20 vents
  • Per-vent charge: $15-$25 each
  • Actual total: $374-$649

Many homeowners do not realize this until the technician is already in their home counting vents. At that point, you feel committed and end up paying far more than you expected.

2. Flat-Rate Pricing (Transparent)

A flat-rate model gives you one price that covers everything — furnace, ducts, and all vents. At Home Pros Group, our flat rate is $345 for a complete furnace and duct cleaning, regardless of how many vents you have.

For a home with 15-20 vents, our flat rate saves you $30-$300+ compared to per-vent competitors. For larger homes and acreages with 25+ vents, the savings are even more dramatic.

What Should Be Included in Your Duct Cleaning Price?

At a minimum, a professional duct cleaning should include:

  • Cleaning of all supply ducts (hot air vents)
  • Cleaning of all return ducts (cold air vents)
  • Main trunk line cleaning
  • Branch line cleaning
  • Register cleaning

A comprehensive service — like what Home Pros Group provides — should also include furnace component cleaning: burners, blower, motor, flame sensor, and furnace cabinet. Some companies charge extra for the furnace or skip it entirely.

Red Flags to Watch For

When getting duct cleaning quotes in Stony Plain or Spruce Grove, watch out for:

  • "$99 whole house" deals: These almost always have hidden per-vent charges or significant upsells once the technician arrives.
  • No on-site assessment: A reputable company should ask about your home size, number of vents, and system type before quoting.
  • Pressure to add services: Be wary of companies that "discover" urgent problems during cleaning and push expensive add-ons.
  • No clear scope of work: Always confirm exactly what is included before work begins.
  • Unusually low prices: If it sounds too good to be true, it usually is.

Average Duct Cleaning Costs in Parkland County (2026)

Based on current market rates in the Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County area:

  • Budget per-vent companies: $350-$500 (after per-vent charges)
  • Mid-range companies: $400-$600
  • Premium companies: $500-$800+
  • Home Pros Group flat rate: $345 (everything included)

How to Get the Best Value

The best value in duct cleaning is not necessarily the lowest price — it is the best service at a fair, transparent price. Here is what to look for:

  • Flat-rate pricing with no hidden fees
  • Furnace cleaning included (not just ducts)
  • All vents included (supply and return)
  • WCB coverage and proper insurance
  • Strong Google reviews from local homeowners
  • Been in business for several years (not a fly-by-night operation)

Why Home Pros Group Is the Best Value

Call (780) 932-7337 for your free quote. No vent counting. No surprises. Just honest, flat-rate service.

What Affects the Price of Duct Cleaning?

Beyond the pricing model (flat rate vs. per-vent), several factors can influence what you pay for duct cleaning in Stony Plain and Spruce Grove:

  • Home size and number of vents: Larger homes with more vents cost more at per-vent companies. With Home Pros Group's flat rate, it does not matter.
  • Duct condition: Some companies charge extra for heavily soiled ducts. We never do — our $345 price applies regardless of how dirty the system is.
  • Accessibility: If vents are behind built-in furniture or in unusual locations, some companies charge access fees. We include all access in our flat rate.
  • Furnace type: Standard gas furnaces, electric furnaces, and dual-fuel systems may be priced differently by some companies. Our rate covers all residential furnace types.
  • Season: Some companies charge a premium during peak season (September-November). Our pricing stays the same year-round.

How to Compare Quotes Fairly

When comparing duct cleaning quotes in Stony Plain or Spruce Grove, make sure you are comparing apples to apples. Ask each company to provide a total, all-in price that includes: furnace cleaning (some companies exclude this) — professional cleaning also directly improves indoor air quality per Health Canada, all supply vents, all return vents, main trunk lines, any access ports needed, and travel fees (some charge extra for rural or acreage properties).

When you compare on these terms, you will find that Home Pros Group's $345 flat rate is consistently among the best value in Parkland County. We include everything with no additional charges.

Is Cheap Duct Cleaning Worth It?

We occasionally hear from homeowners who went with a $99 deal and were disappointed. Common complaints include: the technician was in and out in 30 minutes (a thorough cleaning takes 2-3 hours), only some vents were cleaned, the furnace was not touched, or surprise charges appeared on the final bill.

Good duct cleaning takes time, proper equipment, and skilled technicians. If the price seems impossibly low, it usually means corners are being cut. Our $345 rate reflects what it genuinely costs to do the job right — and we have been doing it at this quality level since 2005.

title: Best Time for Furnace Cleaning in Alberta: Seasonal Guide for Homeowners

content:
When Is the Best Time to Get Your Furnace Cleaned in Alberta?

One of the most common questions we hear from homeowners in Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County is: "When should I get my furnace cleaned?" The answer depends on your priorities, but there are clear advantages to scheduling at certain times of year.

The Best Time: Late Summer to Early Fall (August-October)

For most Alberta homeowners, the ideal time for furnace cleaning is late summer or early fall. Here is why:

  • You are ahead of the rush. Once temperatures drop in October and November, every HVAC company in the Edmonton area gets flooded with calls. By booking in August or September, you get your preferred appointment time and avoid the scramble.
  • Your system is clean before heating season. Starting the heating season with a freshly cleaned furnace means you get maximum efficiency from day one. No wasted energy pushing air through dirty ducts during the first cold snap.
  • Issues get caught early. If our technicians spot a potential problem during cleaning, you have time to address it before you actually need the furnace running at full capacity.
  • Better air quality from the start. Instead of your family breathing recirculated dust from last winter for the first few weeks of heating season, you start with clean ducts and clean air.

Second Best: Spring (April-May)

Spring cleaning is the second most popular time, and for good reason:

  • Remove everything from winter. Six to seven months of continuous furnace operation leaves a lot of buildup in your ducts. Spring cleaning removes everything that accumulated during winter.
  • Prepare for allergy season. With a clean system, you are not recirculating pollen and other allergens through your home during Alberta's spring allergy season.
  • Good availability. Spring is a moderate-demand period, so scheduling is usually easy.

What About Winter Cleaning?

Can you get your furnace cleaned in the middle of winter? Absolutely. We clean furnaces year-round at Home Pros Group. The furnace does need to be shut off during cleaning (typically 2-3 hours), which some homeowners worry about during January or February. In practice, your home will not get uncomfortably cold during this time, especially during the middle of the day.

Winter cleaning is better than no cleaning at all. If it has been more than a year and you suddenly remember in December, do not wait until spring — book it now.

The Worst Time: Never

The only truly bad time for furnace cleaning is never. We have seen homes where the furnace had not been cleaned in 5, 10, even 15+ years. The amount of buildup in these systems is staggering — and the homeowners are typically paying hundreds of dollars extra per year in energy costs without realizing it.

How to Build a Cleaning Routine

The easiest approach is to pick a month and stick with it every year. Here is what we recommend for Alberta homeowners:

  • September: Our top recommendation. Clean system before heating season, good availability, moderate weather.
  • April: Great alternative. Clean out winter buildup, prepare for summer.
  • Any month: Honestly, any month works. The most important thing is that you do it once a year.

Pair It with Other Maintenance

Many of our customers in Stony Plain and Spruce Grove schedule their furnace cleaning alongside other annual home maintenance tasks:

  • Furnace filter replacement (every 3 months, but check during cleaning)
  • Dryer vent cleaning (annual — fire prevention)
  • Gutter cleaning (spring and fall)
  • Carbon monoxide detector battery check
  • Smoke detector battery check

Book Your Cleaning with Home Pros Group

Whatever time of year works for you, Home Pros Group is here with our $345 flat-rate furnace and duct cleaning. Every vent included, no hidden fees. We serve Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, Parkland County, and Greater Edmonton.

Call (780) 932-7337 to schedule your appointment. We recommend booking 1-2 weeks in advance during our busy seasons (September-November and March-April).

What to Expect During Your Cleaning Appointment

Knowing what happens during a furnace cleaning helps you plan your day and prepare your home. Here is a typical Home Pros Group appointment from start to finish:

Before We Arrive

  • Clear the area around your furnace (about 3 feet of space on all sides)
  • Make sure all vents are accessible (move furniture away from vents if possible)
  • Secure pets in a safe room — we will be using equipment that may be loud

During the Cleaning (2-3 Hours)

After the Cleaning

Your system is ready to run immediately after cleaning. You may notice improved airflow right away. Some homeowners report a slight dust smell for the first hour or two after cleaning as the system circulates for the first time — this is normal and goes away quickly.

Booking Tips for the Best Experience

title: Do I Really Need Duct Cleaning? Honest Advice from a Local HVAC Cleaning Company
excerpt: Is duct cleaning worth it? An honest assessment from a local cleaning company about when you truly need it, when you can wait, and the real benefits of professional duct cleaning.
author: Home Pros Team
meta_title: Do I Really Need Duct Cleaning? Honest Advice | Home Pros Group
meta_description: Is duct cleaning worth it? Honest, no-BS advice from a local HVAC cleaning company on when you need it, when you can wait, and the real benefits.
content:
Do I Really Need Duct Cleaning? An Honest Assessment

We are going to be straight with you. As a duct cleaning company, we obviously benefit when you book a cleaning. But we have been in business since 2005 because we give honest advice — even when it means telling someone they do not need our services right now.

Here is the truth about when you need duct cleaning — backed by Health Canada indoor air quality research, when you do not, and how to make the right decision for your home.

When You Definitely Need Duct Cleaning

There are situations where professional duct cleaning is clearly necessary:

  • Visible mold growth inside ducts: If you can see mold on the inside of your ducts or on vent registers, get them cleaned immediately. Mold spores circulating through your HVAC system is a serious health concern.
  • Rodent or insect infestation: If pests have been living in your ductwork, you need professional cleaning to remove droppings, nesting materials, and contamination.
  • After major renovations: Drywall dust, sawdust, and construction debris get everywhere during renovations — including deep into your duct system.
  • Moving into a previously-owned home: You do not know the previous owners' cleaning habits. A fresh cleaning gives you a known baseline.
  • Excessive dust: If you are dusting your home every few days and it keeps coming back, dirty ducts are almost certainly contributing to the problem.

When You Should Strongly Consider It

  • It has been 2+ years since your last cleaning: In Alberta's climate, with 6-7 months of heavy furnace use per year, 2 years of buildup is significant.
  • Anyone in your home has allergies or asthma: Clean ducts noticeably reduce the amount of allergens circulating in your home.
  • You have pets: Pet dander and hair accumulate in ducts faster than you might think, especially with dogs and cats that shed heavily.
  • Your energy bills have been increasing: Dirty ducts restrict airflow, making your furnace work harder and use more energy.
  • Musty or unpleasant odours when the furnace runs: This often indicates significant buildup or even mold in the ductwork.

When You Can Probably Wait

If you cleaned your ducts within the last 12 months, your home is relatively dust-free, nobody has allergies, and you do not have pets, you can probably wait. That said, annual cleaning is still good preventive maintenance — similar to changing your car's oil even when it is running fine.

The Real Benefits of Professional Duct Cleaning

When it is done right, professional duct cleaning delivers measurable benefits:

  • Better air quality: Fewer allergens, less dust, and cleaner air for your family. This is not marketing speak — it is physics. Remove the contaminants from the ducts, and they stop circulating through your home.
  • Energy savings: A clean HVAC system runs more efficiently. Clean ducts mean better airflow, which means your furnace does not have to work as hard. This typically translates to 10-20% savings on heating costs.
  • Longer equipment life: When your furnace does not have to fight through restricted airflow, it runs under less stress. This extends its lifespan and reduces the likelihood of breakdowns.
  • Reduced dust: Many of our customers report noticeably less household dust after cleaning — particularly in the first few months.
  • Odour elimination: Cooking smells, pet odours, and mustiness that have embedded themselves in ductwork get removed at the source.

What About the Studies That Say Duct Cleaning Is Unnecessary?

You may have read articles citing studies that question the benefits of duct cleaning. Here is context: most of those studies were conducted in milder climates where furnaces run 3-4 months per year, not 6-7 months like in Alberta. The amount of contamination that accumulates in an Alberta home's ductwork is significantly higher than in a home in southern Ontario or British Columbia.

In our climate, with our furnace usage, the case for regular duct cleaning is much stronger.

How to Tell If Your Ducts Are Dirty

Here are some practical ways to check:

  • Remove a vent cover and look inside with a flashlight. If you see visible dust or debris, your ducts need cleaning.
  • Hold a white tissue near a supply vent when the furnace is running. If it discolours quickly, there is significant dust in the system.
  • Check your furnace filter. If it is getting dirty faster than expected (before the recommended replacement interval), dirty ducts are likely contributing extra debris.

The Bottom Line

For Alberta homeowners — especially those of us in Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County — annual duct cleaning is a smart investment. If you are on the fence, ask yourself: would you rather breathe clean air or recirculated dust? The answer is usually pretty clear.

Home Pros Group cleans ducts for a $345 flat rate, with every vent included. No per-vent charges, no hidden fees. Call (780) 932-7337 for a free quote.

The Difference Between Duct Cleaning and Air Duct Sealing

Some homeowners confuse duct cleaning with duct sealing. These are two completely different services:

  • Duct cleaning removes accumulated dust, debris, and contaminants from inside your ductwork. This is what Home Pros Group does.
  • Duct sealing closes gaps and leaks in your ductwork to prevent conditioned air from escaping into wall cavities and attics. This is a different service typically done by HVAC contractors.

Both services are valuable, but for different reasons. If you suspect your ducts have leaks (uneven heating, high energy bills despite clean ducts), ask an HVAC technician about sealing. For air quality and efficiency improvements from removing contaminants, duct cleaning is what you need.

A Quick Self-Assessment

Answer these five questions to determine if you need duct cleaning now:

  1. Has it been more than 12 months since your last professional duct cleaning? (Yes = time to book)
  2. Do you notice dust accumulating on surfaces within days of cleaning? (Yes = your ducts are likely contributing)
  3. Does anyone in your home have allergies, asthma, or respiratory issues? (Yes = clean ducts can help significantly)
  4. Do you have pets? (Yes = annual cleaning is strongly recommended)
  5. Have you noticed increased energy bills or uneven heating? (Yes = restricted airflow from dirty ducts may be the cause)

If you answered yes to two or more of these questions, professional duct cleaning is a smart investment. Call Home Pros Group at (780) 932-7337 for an honest assessment and a flat-rate quote.

title: Carbon Monoxide and Your Furnace: What Every Stony Plain Homeowner Needs to Know
excerpt: Carbon monoxide from a poorly maintained furnace is a silent killer. Learn how furnace cleaning prevents CO leaks, the warning signs, and how to keep your Stony Plain family safe.
author: Home Pros Team
meta_title: Carbon Monoxide & Your Furnace: Safety Guide | Home Pros Group
meta_description: Learn how furnace cleaning prevents carbon monoxide leaks. Essential safety information for Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County homeowners.
content:
Carbon Monoxide and Your Furnace: A Critical Safety Guide

Carbon monoxide (CO) is called the "silent killer" for a reason. It is colourless, odourless, and tasteless — you cannot detect it without a CO detector. And one of the most common sources of carbon monoxide in Canadian homes is the furnace.

Every year in Alberta, there are CO-related hospitalizations and deaths linked to poorly maintained heating systems. This is not something to take lightly, especially in Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County where we rely on our furnaces for 6-7 months of the year.

How Furnaces Produce Carbon Monoxide

All gas and propane furnaces produce carbon monoxide as a byproduct of combustion. In a properly functioning furnace, the CO is safely vented outside through the exhaust flue. The problem starts when components get dirty, damaged, or misaligned:

  • Dirty burners: When burners are coated with dust and debris, they cannot burn fuel efficiently. Incomplete combustion produces significantly more CO than clean burners.
  • Cracked heat exchanger: The heat exchanger separates the combustion gases (including CO) from the air that circulates through your home. If it develops cracks — which can happen over time, especially in neglected systems — CO can leak into your living space.
  • Blocked exhaust flue: If the exhaust path is partially blocked, CO cannot vent properly and may back up into the home.
  • Dirty flame sensor: A dirty flame sensor can cause the furnace to cycle improperly, leading to incomplete combustion and increased CO production.

How Furnace Cleaning Helps Prevent CO Issues

Regular professional furnace cleaning is one of the most effective ways to reduce CO risk. During our cleaning, we:

  • Clean all burner components, ensuring efficient combustion
  • Clean the flame sensor, ensuring proper furnace operation
  • Clean the blower and motor, ensuring proper air circulation
  • Visually inspect accessible portions of the heat exchanger for visible damage
  • Check that the system is operating properly before we leave

While our cleaning is not a substitute for a full mechanical inspection by an HVAC technician, it addresses many of the maintenance factors that contribute to CO production.

Warning Signs of Carbon Monoxide in Your Home

Know these warning signs — they could save your life:

  • Headaches, especially when the furnace is running
  • Dizziness, nausea, or confusion
  • Flu-like symptoms that improve when you leave the house
  • Yellow or flickering pilot light (should be blue)
  • Soot or discolouration around the furnace
  • Excessive condensation on windows near the furnace
  • A strange, burning smell when the furnace runs

If multiple family members are experiencing headaches or flu-like symptoms at the same time — especially if symptoms improve when away from home — take it seriously. Open windows, leave the house, and call 911 or your gas company.

Essential CO Safety Equipment

Every home in Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, and Parkland County should have:

  • CO detectors on every level of the home — Install them near bedrooms and near the furnace. Test batteries monthly and replace detectors every 5-7 years.
  • A working smoke detector in every bedroom — CO detectors and smoke detectors serve different functions. You need both.
  • An emergency plan — Everyone in the household should know what to do if the CO detector goes off: get out, stay out, and call 911.

How Often Should You Have Your Furnace Inspected?

We recommend two types of regular maintenance:

  • Annual furnace cleaning: This is what Home Pros Group provides. We clean all accessible components and perform a visual inspection. Cost: $345 flat rate.
  • Annual mechanical inspection: An HVAC technician should inspect your furnace mechanically, including a thorough heat exchanger check, gas pressure test, and combustion analysis. This is a separate service from cleaning.

Think of cleaning and inspection as two complementary services. Cleaning keeps the system running efficiently and reduces CO production. Mechanical inspection catches structural issues like heat exchanger cracks that cleaning alone cannot address.

Your Family's Safety Is Worth It

Furnace maintenance is not optional — it is essential for your family's safety. The cost of annual cleaning ($345 with Home Pros Group) is nothing compared to the potential consequences of a CO leak.

Call us at (780) 932-7337 to schedule your furnace cleaning. We serve Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, Parkland County, and the Greater Edmonton area.

Alberta-Specific CO Risks

Alberta homeowners face unique carbon monoxide risks that people in milder climates do not think about:

  • Extended furnace operation: Our furnaces run 6-7 months per year, much longer than homes in southern Canada. More operating hours means more opportunity for CO-producing issues to develop.
  • Multiple combustion appliances: Many Alberta homes have a gas furnace, gas water heater, and possibly a gas fireplace. Each is a potential CO source.
  • Sealed homes: Modern Alberta homes are built tight for energy efficiency. While this saves on heating, it also means there is less natural ventilation to dilute any CO that enters the living space.
  • Extreme cold reducing ventilation: During -30 degree cold snaps, homeowners naturally keep windows sealed tight and may not run bathroom fans or range hoods as often. This reduces air exchange and can allow CO to accumulate.

What to Do If Your CO Detector Goes Off

  1. Do not ignore it. A beeping CO detector is a potential life-threatening emergency.
  2. Open windows immediately if you can do so safely.
  3. Get everyone (including pets) out of the house.
  4. Call 911 from outside the home.
  5. Do not go back inside until emergency responders say it is safe.
  6. Call your gas company (ATCO Gas in most of Parkland County: 1-800-511-3447).

Even if the alarm stops, do not assume the danger has passed. CO levels can fluctuate, and intermittent exposure is still dangerous.

Protect Your Family

Carbon monoxide prevention starts with regular furnace maintenance. A clean, well-maintained furnace produces less CO and vents it properly. Home Pros Group provides thorough furnace cleaning that addresses the key maintenance factors contributing to CO production.

Schedule your annual furnace cleaning today. Call (780) 932-7337. Serving Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, Parkland County, and Greater Edmonton.

title: New Home? Why You Need Duct Cleaning Before Moving In (Even in a New Build)
excerpt: Just bought a new home in Spruce Grove or Stony Plain? Whether it is a brand-new build or a resale, here is why duct cleaning before moving in is one of the smartest investments you can make.
author: Home Pros Team

meta_description: Just bought a new home? Why duct cleaning before move-in is essential — even in new builds. Expert advice for Stony Plain and Spruce Grove homebuyers.
content:
Why Your New Home Needs Duct Cleaning — Yes, Even New Builds

Congratulations on your new home! Whether you just purchased a brand-new build in one of Spruce Grove's growing communities like Greenbury or Harvest Ridge, or you are moving into a resale home in Stony Plain, one of the first things you should do is schedule a professional duct cleaning. Here is why — supported by Health Canada's indoor air quality guidelines.

New Construction Homes

You might think a brand-new home would have clean ducts. After all, nobody has lived there yet, right? Unfortunately, that is not the case. During construction, your duct system is exposed to an enormous amount of debris:

  • Drywall dust: This fine white powder gets everywhere during construction, including deep into your ductwork. It is nearly impossible to avoid.
  • Sawdust and wood shavings: From framing, trim work, and cabinet installation.
  • Insulation fibres: Fibreglass insulation particles can become airborne during installation and settle in ducts.
  • Paint fumes and overspray: Residue from interior painting can settle in the duct system.
  • General construction debris: Screws, drywall scraps, coffee cups — we have found all sorts of things in new construction ductwork.

Many builders do a basic "blow-out" of the ducts before handing over the keys, but this is not the same as a professional cleaning. A proper cleaning with professional-grade equipment removes what a quick blow-out leaves behind.

Resale Homes

For previously-owned homes, duct cleaning before move-in is even more important — NADCA specifically recommends cleaning after any change of occupancy:

  • You do not know the previous owner's cleaning history. The ducts could have years of accumulated dust, pet dander, and contaminants. Some homes we clean have never had their ducts serviced.
  • Previous pet owners. Even if you do not have pets, the previous owners might have had dogs, cats, or other animals. Pet dander embeds itself in ductwork and will circulate through your home with every cycle.
  • Smokers. Cigarette and cannabis smoke residue in ducts is persistent and will continue to off-gas for months without professional cleaning.
  • Unknown allergens. If anyone in your family has allergies, starting with clean ducts eliminates a major source of potential triggers.
  • Peace of mind. Moving into a home with professionally cleaned ducts means you know exactly what you are breathing from day one.

The Best Time to Clean: Before You Move In

Scheduling duct cleaning before you move furniture in is ideal because:

  • Our technicians have clear access to every vent without navigating around furniture
  • Any dust dislodged during cleaning will not settle on your belongings
  • The job gets done faster and more efficiently
  • You start your first night in the new home breathing clean air

If you have already moved in, no worries — we clean occupied homes every day. But if you have the option to schedule before move-in day, take it.

What About the Builder's Warranty?

Professional duct cleaning will not void your builder's warranty. In fact, many builders recommend it. Our cleaning process is non-invasive — we use vacuum and agitation methods that clean without damaging your ductwork. We do not perform any mechanical work on the furnace itself.

Spruce Grove and Stony Plain New Builds

Spruce Grove is one of the fastest-growing cities in Alberta, with new communities going up constantly. Stony Plain is also seeing steady development. We regularly clean ducts in brand-new homes throughout both communities, and the amount of construction debris we remove is always eye-opening for new homeowners.

If you are buying a new build from any of the major builders in the area, add duct cleaning to your post-possession checklist. It is a small investment that makes a big difference in your home's air quality from day one.

What We Include

Our $345 flat-rate cleaning covers everything:

  • Complete furnace component cleaning
  • Every supply and return duct in your home
  • All register covers
  • Main trunk lines and all branch lines
  • Visual inspection of the entire system

No per-vent charges. No hidden fees. One flat rate, regardless of home size.

Schedule Your Pre-Move-In Cleaning

Home Pros Group serves Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, Parkland County, and Greater Edmonton. Call (780) 932-7337 to schedule your cleaning. We can often accommodate appointments within a few days, so even if your possession date is coming up fast, give us a call.

Common Questions About New Home Duct Cleaning

Will duct cleaning void my new home warranty?

No. Professional duct cleaning is non-invasive and will not void your builder's warranty. In fact, many builders recommend post-construction duct cleaning. Our process uses vacuum and agitation — we do not perform any mechanical work on the furnace itself.

My builder said they cleaned the ducts. Is that enough?

Most builders do a basic blow-out, which removes some loose debris but does not provide the thorough cleaning that professional equipment delivers. Think of it as the difference between quickly sweeping a floor and deep-cleaning it. Both help, but the deep clean gets what the sweep misses.

Can I save money by doing it myself?

You can clean individual vent registers yourself with a vacuum and brush. However, you cannot effectively clean the main trunk lines, branch ducts, and deep ductwork without professional equipment. For a new home, we strongly recommend professional cleaning to ensure all construction debris is fully removed.

How soon after possession should I schedule?

Ideally, schedule before moving furniture in. If that is not possible, within the first week of moving in is best. The sooner you clean, the less construction dust your family breathes and the less settles on your belongings.

A Clean Start for Your New Home

Buying a new home — whether brand new or resale — is exciting. Starting with professionally cleaned ducts gives you peace of mind that your family is breathing clean air from day one. At $345 for a complete cleaning with every vent included, it is a small investment that makes a big difference.

Moving to Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, or anywhere in Parkland County? Welcome to the neighbourhood. Call Home Pros Group at (780) 932-7337 to schedule your pre-move-in duct cleaning.

title: Pet Owners: How Dogs and Cats Affect Your Duct System (And What to Do About It)
excerpt: Love your pets but hate the dust? Learn how pet hair and dander affect your HVAC system, why pet owners need more frequent duct cleaning, and tips to keep your air clean.
author: Home Pros Team
meta_title: Pet Owners: How Dogs & Cats Affect Your Ducts | Home Pros Group
meta_description: How pet hair and dander affect your HVAC system and indoor air quality. Expert tips for pet owners on duct cleaning frequency and air quality improvement.
content:
How Your Furry Friends Are Affecting Your Home's Air Quality

We love our pets — and clearly so does Parkland County. Walk through any Stony Plain or Spruce Grove neighbourhood and you will see dogs in every other yard. Cats are just as popular. But as much as we love our pets, they create unique challenges for our home HVAC systems and indoor air quality.

What Pets Put Into Your Air

Dogs and cats contribute several contaminants to your indoor air:

  • Pet dander: Tiny flakes of skin that are shed constantly. Dander is microscopic — you cannot see it, but it circulates freely through your ductwork. It is also one of the most common allergens.
  • Pet hair/fur: Obvious and visible, pet hair gets sucked into your return vents and accumulates in the duct system. During shedding season (spring and fall), the volume increases dramatically.
  • Saliva particles: Dried saliva from grooming becomes airborne and circulates through the HVAC system.
  • Outdoor contaminants: Dogs track in pollen, dirt, grass, and other outdoor particles that eventually make their way into the ductwork.

What Happens Inside Your Ducts

When pet hair and dander enter your duct system, here is what happens:

  • Hair collects at bends and joints in the ductwork, creating clumps that restrict airflow
  • Dander coats the interior surfaces of ducts, building up over time
  • Every time the furnace cycles, these contaminants get recirculated through your home — 5-7 times per day
  • The furnace filter catches some of it, but not all. Dander particles are small enough to pass through many standard filters
  • Over months, the buildup becomes significant enough to impact airflow, efficiency, and air quality

How Often Should Pet Owners Get Ducts Cleaned?

For homes with pets, we recommend:

  • One dog or cat: Annual cleaning (same as the general recommendation, but more important to stick to the schedule)
  • Multiple pets: Every 8-10 months, or twice a year if you have 3+ pets
  • Heavy shedders (Huskies, German Shepherds, Golden Retrievers, etc.): Every 8-10 months
  • Pet + allergy sufferer in the home: Every 8-10 months minimum

Tips for Pet Owners Between Cleanings

Between professional cleanings, there are steps you can take to minimize the impact of pets on your air quality:

  • Upgrade your furnace filter: Use a MERV 11 or MERV 13 filter instead of the basic MERV 8 that most homes use. Higher MERV ratings capture smaller particles like pet dander. Note: check that your system can handle the increased airflow resistance of a higher MERV filter.
  • Change filters more frequently: Pet owners should change their furnace filter every 2-3 months instead of the typical 3-4 months.
  • Groom pets regularly: Regular brushing (ideally outdoors) removes loose hair before it enters the air. During shedding season, daily brushing makes a noticeable difference.
  • Vacuum frequently: Use a vacuum with a HEPA filter. Vacuum at least twice a week, paying special attention to areas near return vents.
  • Keep vents clear: Make sure furniture and pet beds are not blocking supply or return vents. Blocked vents create dead zones where pet hair accumulates.
  • Consider an air purifier: A quality HEPA air purifier in your main living area can supplement your HVAC filtration and capture airborne pet dander.

The Difference Clean Ducts Make for Pet Owners

Pet owners consistently report the most dramatic improvement after professional duct cleaning. The reduction in dust, the improvement in air freshness, and the decrease in allergy symptoms are all more noticeable in homes with pets because there was more contamination to begin with.

One of our Stony Plain customers with three dogs told us: "I did not realize how much pet hair was in our ducts until I saw what came out. My daughter's allergy symptoms improved within days of the cleaning."

Our Flat-Rate Advantage for Pet Owners

Here is something pet owners especially appreciate about Home Pros Group: our $345 flat rate covers every vent in your home, no matter what comes out of them. Some per-vent companies have been known to charge extra for "heavily contaminated" ducts — which pet owners' ducts usually are. We never do that. $345, every time, regardless of condition.

Call Home Pros Group at (780) 932-7337 to schedule your cleaning. We serve pet-loving families across Stony Plain, Spruce Grove, Parkland County, and Greater Edmonton.

Which Pets Create the Most HVAC Contamination?

Not all pets are equal when it comes to HVAC impact. Here is a rough ranking from most to least contamination:

  1. Heavy-shedding dogs (Huskies, German Shepherds, Golden Retrievers, Labs) — these breeds produce enormous amounts of hair and dander, especially during spring and fall shedding seasons.
  2. Cats — cat dander is particularly potent and spreads easily through ductwork. Indoor cats contribute more than outdoor cats since they spend all their time inside.
  3. Medium-shedding dogs (Beagles, Bulldogs, most mixed breeds) — moderate hair and dander, still significant over a year.
  4. Low-shedding dogs (Poodles, Schnauzers, Bichons) — produce less hair but still generate dander.
  5. Small animals (hamsters, rabbits, guinea pigs) — if their cage is in a room with a vent, bedding material and dander enter the duct system.
  6. Birds — feather dust and dander are lighter and spread further through ductwork than you might expect.

The Financial Case for Regular Cleaning with Pets

Pet owners who skip annual duct cleaning — which NADCA recommends every 2-3 years for pet households — pay for it in other ways: higher energy bills from restricted airflow (pet hair clumps at duct bends), more frequent furnace filter replacements, faster furnace wear from working against contaminated ducts, higher allergy medication costs for sensitive family members, and more frequent house cleaning due to recirculating dust and dander.

At $345 per year for a complete cleaning, the investment pays for itself many times over. Your HVAC system runs better, your home is cleaner, and your family breathes easier.

Call Home Pros Group at (780) 932-7337. We love pets — and we love helping pet owners breathe cleaner air.
